sandbox.name: RangedDamage
sandbox.label: Ranged Damage
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for the damage you deal with ranged weapons such as rifles, pistols, and bows. Higher = more damage at distance.
sandbox.example: Auf Insane teilt man ohnehin nur reduzierten Schaden aus, dieser Wert skaliert das zusaetzlich.

sandbox.name: MeleeDamage
sandbox.label: Melee Damage
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for your melee damage with fists, clubs, sledgehammers, and blades. Higher = harder-hitting close-combat strikes.

sandbox.name: BlockDamage
sandbox.label: Block Damage
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for the damage you deal to blocks and structures when mining or tearing buildings down. Higher = faster demolition of POIs and walls.

sandbox.name: TerrainDamage
sandbox.label: Terrain Damage
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for your damage against terrain when digging or mining. Higher = faster removal of dirt, stone, and ore veins.

sandbox.name: HeadshotMultiplier
sandbox.label: Headshot Multiplier
sandbox.desc: Bonus damage applied to headshots against enemies. Higher = headshots kill noticeably faster, rewarding precise aim.
sandbox.example: Auf Insane/Nightmare sind Headshots ohnehin Pflicht, dieser Wert verstaerkt ihren Vorteil.

sandbox.name: CrouchSpeed
sandbox.label: Crouch Speed
sandbox.desc: Your movement speed while crouched in sneak mode. Higher = faster sneaking, letting you reposition while staying stealthy.

sandbox.name: WalkSpeed
sandbox.label: Walk Speed
sandbox.desc: Your normal movement speed when not sprinting. Higher = faster walking across the map.

sandbox.name: RunSpeed
sandbox.label: Run Speed
sandbox.desc: Your sprint speed. Higher = faster running, which matters for outrunning Nightmare-speed sprinters.

sandbox.name: JumpStrength
sandbox.label: Jump Strength
sandbox.desc: How high and far your jumps reach. Higher = the player jumps farther and higher.

sandbox.name: StaminaUsage
sandbox.label: Stamina Usage
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how much stamina actions like sprinting, attacking, and mining drain. Higher = stamina empties faster, forcing more rests.
sandbox.example: Hoher Wert macht Nahkampf/Sprint anstrengender, Cardio-Perk wird wichtiger.

sandbox.name: StaminaRegen
sandbox.label: Stamina Regeneration
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how fast stamina refills. Higher = quicker recovery between actions, so you can keep fighting or mining sooner.

sandbox.name: PlayerLevelBonusApplied
sandbox.label: Level Stat Bonus
sandbox.desc: Toggles whether a bonus scaling with your player level is applied to stats such as damage. On = higher level grants extra stat boosts on top of perks.

sandbox.name: JarRefund
sandbox.label: Jar Refund
sandbox.desc: The share of empty jars or containers returned when drinking or cooking. Higher = you lose jars less often, easing the glass shortage.

sandbox.name: ShowHealthBars
sandbox.label: Enemy Health Bars
sandbox.desc: Shows or hides health bars above enemies. On = you can see how much health a target has left.

sandbox.name: ShowEnemyDamage
sandbox.label: Show Damage Numbers
sandbox.desc: Shows floating damage numbers when you hit enemies. On = popup hit values appear during combat.

sandbox.name: NewbieCoat
sandbox.label: New Player Protection
sandbox.desc: A grace period for new or freshly respawned players during which they take reduced or no damage. Prevents instant death right after spawning.

sandbox.name: HeadshotMode
sandbox.label: Headshot Mode
sandbox.desc: Sets the rules for when a hit counts as a headshot, or whether only headshots can kill. Stricter settings make headshots the main way to drop enemies.

sandbox.name: IncomingDamage
sandbox.label: Incoming Player Damage
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for the damage you take from enemies. Higher = you die faster, so this is one of the strongest difficulty levers in the game.
sandbox.example: Auf Insane nimmt man bereits stark erhoehten Schaden, ein hoher Wert hier ist brutal.

sandbox.name: XPMultiplier
sandbox.label: XP Multiplier
sandbox.desc: Scales all experience points you earn. Higher = faster leveling, which also raises your gamestage and the strength of enemies and loot more quickly.

sandbox.name: ShowXP
sandbox.label: Show XP
sandbox.desc: Shows XP gain numbers and/or the XP progress bar on screen. Pure display option with no effect on gameplay.

sandbox.name: EncumbranceModifier
sandbox.label: Encumbrance Penalty
sandbox.desc: Controls the movement slowdown caused by a full or overloaded inventory. Higher = a heavier speed penalty when your backpack is stuffed.

sandbox.name: ItemDegradation
sandbox.label: Item Degradation
sandbox.desc: Controls how fast weapons, tools and armor wear out with use. Higher = gear breaks sooner and needs repairs more often.

sandbox.name: LoseItemsOnDeathType
sandbox.label: Item Loss On Death (Type)
sandbox.desc: Defines which items you lose when you die (e.g. everything, backpack only, toolbelt only, or nothing). Sets how harsh the death penalty is in terms of lost gear.
sandbox.example: Nicks Welt: Drop On Death = Everything, also Totalverlust.

sandbox.name: LoseItemsOnDeathCount
sandbox.label: Item Loss On Death (Count)
sandbox.desc: Number of items or slots lost on death, unless the loss type already removes everything. Higher = more lost per death.

sandbox.name: DegradeItemsOnDeath
sandbox.label: Degrade Items On Death
sandbox.desc: Toggles whether dying also wears down the quality and durability of the gear you were carrying. On = each death damages your equipment.

sandbox.name: DegradeAmountOnDeath
sandbox.label: Death Degradation Amount
sandbox.desc: How much wear is applied to your gear per death when death degradation is enabled. Higher = a bigger quality loss each time you die.

sandbox.name: DeathPenalty
sandbox.label: Death Penalty
sandbox.desc: Sets the general penalty for dying beyond lost items: no penalty, an XP penalty only, an injured debuff, or permadeath. Determines how punishing each death is. (Permadeath ends the character.)

sandbox.name: DropOnDeath
sandbox.label: Drop On Death
sandbox.desc: Controls what your body drops when you die: nothing, backpack, backpack plus toolbelt, everything, or delete it all. Determines whether you can return to recover your loot at the death spot.
sandbox.example: Nicks Welt: Everything = beim Tod fallen alle Items, ohne PvP-Schutz auch fuer Mitspieler greifbar.

sandbox.name: DropOnQuit
sandbox.label: Drop On Quit
sandbox.desc: Controls what a player drops when they log out. Set to Nothing to stop players from securing or losing their loot by quitting during PvP.

sandbox.name: InfectionRate
sandbox.label: Infection Rate
sandbox.desc: How often zombie hits give you an infection and how fast it worsens toward death. Higher = infected more often and the timer runs out faster, forcing more antibiotics and honey.

sandbox.name: EnemySpawnMode
sandbox.label: Enemy Spawning
sandbox.desc: Master switch for all enemy spawns. Off = no zombies or hostile animals anywhere, turning the world into a build/peaceful mode.

sandbox.name: EntityDamage
sandbox.label: Damage Taken From Enemies
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for damage zombies and animals deal to you. Higher = enemies hit much harder and you go down faster.

sandbox.name: BlockDamageAI
sandbox.label: Enemy Block Damage
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how much damage zombies do to blocks and structures outside Blood Moon. Higher = they tear through your base walls faster.

sandbox.name: BlockDamageAIBM
sandbox.label: Blood Moon Block Damage
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for zombie block damage during the Blood Moon horde. Higher = the horde night punishes your base far more, so reinforce before night 7.
sandbox.example: Nicks Welt: 75% (AI Blood Moon Block Damage).

sandbox.name: ZombieMove
sandbox.label: Zombie Speed (Day)
sandbox.desc: Movement speed of normal zombies during the day, from walking up to Nightmare sprint. Higher = far more aggressive, harder-to-escape daytime enemies.
sandbox.example: Nicks Welt: Nightmare = Zombies sprinten auch tagsueber.

sandbox.name: ZombieMoveNight
sandbox.label: Zombie Speed (Night)
sandbox.desc: Movement speed of normal zombies at night. Higher = nights become much deadlier; Nightmare means they sprint relentlessly in the dark.

sandbox.name: ZombieFeralMove
sandbox.label: Feral Zombie Speed
sandbox.desc: Movement speed of feral (tougher, glowing-eyed) zombies. Higher = ferals are nearly impossible to outrun once they spot you.

sandbox.name: ZombieBMMove
sandbox.label: Blood Moon Zombie Speed
sandbox.desc: Movement speed of zombies during the Blood Moon horde. Higher = horde-night enemies storm your base faster, leaving less time to react.

sandbox.name: ZombieFeralSense
sandbox.label: Feral Sense
sandbox.desc: When feral senses are active (Off, Day, Night, or All), making zombies far better at detecting and hunting you. The more it's on, the harder stealth and hiding become.

sandbox.name: AISmellMode
sandbox.label: AI Smell Sensitivity
sandbox.desc: How strongly zombies can detect and track you by scent. Higher = stealth and hiding are much harder because enemies sniff you out.

sandbox.name: AllowZombieDigging
sandbox.label: Zombie Digging
sandbox.desc: Whether zombies can dig through terrain to reach you. On = underground and buried bases are no longer safe from the horde.

sandbox.name: ZombieRageChance
sandbox.label: Zombie Rage Chance
sandbox.desc: Chance that a zombie you hit flips into a rage state, briefly moving faster and more aggressively. Higher = more frequent sudden sprint bursts mid-fight.

sandbox.name: EntityIncomingDamage
sandbox.label: Damage Dealt To Enemies
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for damage enemies and animals take from you. Higher = zombies and wildlife die faster.
sandbox.example: (Wirkung aus Namen abgeleitet) Gegenstueck zu EntityDamage.

sandbox.name: MaxEnemyTier
sandbox.label: Max Enemy Tier
sandbox.desc: Caps the highest enemy tier/strength that can spawn, such as how far feral and radiated variants appear. Higher = tougher enemy types allowed in the world.

sandbox.name: BiomeZombieRespawn
sandbox.label: Biome Zombie Respawn
sandbox.desc: Whether and how fast zombies repopulate a biome after you clear an area. Higher = the world refills with zombies more quickly.

sandbox.name: BiomeAnimalRespawn
sandbox.label: Biome Animal Respawn
sandbox.desc: Whether and how fast wild animals repopulate the biomes. Higher = more game to hunt; lower = meat and leather get scarce.

sandbox.name: BiomeEnemyDensity
sandbox.label: Biome Enemy Density
sandbox.desc: How many enemies can roam an open-world biome at once. Higher = more zombies wandering each area, raising constant outdoor danger.
sandbox.example: In Kombination mit Nightmare-Speed wird Reisen sehr gefaehrlich.

sandbox.name: ZombiesEatAnimals
sandbox.label: Zombies Eat Animals
sandbox.desc: Sets whether zombies hunt and kill wild animals. When On, animals can be thinned out by zombies before you get a chance to hunt them yourself.

sandbox.name: BloodMoonFrequency
sandbox.label: Blood Moon Frequency
sandbox.desc: Number of days between Blood Moon horde nights. Lower = more frequent hordes; set to Disabled to turn Blood Moon off entirely.

sandbox.name: BloodMoonRange
sandbox.label: Blood Moon Day Variance
sandbox.desc: Random spread, in days, by which a Blood Moon can land earlier or later than the fixed cycle. 0 = perfectly predictable; higher = harder to plan around.

sandbox.name: BloodMoonWarning
sandbox.label: Blood Moon Warning
sandbox.desc: Whether and when you get a heads-up on the day a Blood Moon night will occur, so you can prep your base and gear in time.
sandbox.example: z.B. Warnung am Morgen, Abend oder gar keine Warnung

sandbox.name: BloodMoonEnemyCount
sandbox.label: Blood Moon Horde Size
sandbox.desc: Max number of zombies alive per player during a Blood Moon. Higher = a far deadlier horde night that demands a sturdier base and more ammo.
sandbox.example: Sehr hohe Werte koennen schwache PCs und die Basis ueberlasten

sandbox.name: AirDropFrequency
sandbox.label: Air Drop Frequency
sandbox.desc: How many in-game hours pass between parachute supply crate drops. Shorter intervals = more frequent free loot from the sky; can be disabled entirely.

sandbox.name: AirDropMarker
sandbox.label: Air Drop Marker
sandbox.desc: Toggles the map/compass marker on supply crates. Off = you must spot the parachute in the sky and find the landing yourself.

sandbox.name: AirDropRandomTime
sandbox.label: Randomize Air Drop Time
sandbox.desc: When on, air drops arrive at random times of day instead of fixed hours, making them less predictable.

sandbox.name: BiomeProgression
sandbox.label: Biome Progression
sandbox.desc: Ties loot quality and enemy strength to the biome: harsher biomes (burnt, wasteland) give better loot but tougher zombies. Off = difficulty is the same everywhere.

sandbox.name: TemperatureSurvival
sandbox.label: Temperature Survival
sandbox.desc: Enables the heat/cold mechanic where extreme temperatures affect your character, forcing you to dress for the weather and manage heating/cooling. Off = temperature is ignored.

sandbox.name: StormFreq
sandbox.label: Storm Frequency
sandbox.desc: How often the V3.0 storms roll in. Higher = more frequent bad weather with reduced visibility and weather effects; 0 disables storms.

sandbox.name: StormWarning
sandbox.label: Storm Warning
sandbox.desc: Toggles an advance warning before incoming storms so you can reach shelter in time.

sandbox.name: HeatMapSensitivity
sandbox.label: Heat Map Sensitivity
sandbox.desc: How strongly your activity (forging, generators, gunfire) builds heat on the heat map that attracts Screamers and extra zombies. Higher = noise draws enemies faster.
sandbox.example: Hohe Empfindlichkeit lockt mehr Screamer zu deiner Basis

sandbox.name: GlobalGSModifier
sandbox.label: Global Gamestage Multiplier
sandbox.desc: Global multiplier on gamestage, which drives the whole world's difficulty and loot level. Higher = enemies and loot scale up with you faster.
sandbox.example: Stark erhoeht eskaliert die Schwierigkeit sehr frueh

sandbox.name: BiomeGSModifier
sandbox.label: Biome Gamestage Multiplier
sandbox.desc: Per-biome multiplier on gamestage that makes harsher biomes extra dangerous. Works together with Biome Progression.

sandbox.name: GlobalLSModifier
sandbox.label: Global Loot Stage Multiplier
sandbox.desc: Global multiplier on loot stage, and thus the quality of loot you find. Higher = better-tier items start dropping earlier.

sandbox.name: BiomeLSModifier
sandbox.label: Biome Loot Stage Multiplier
sandbox.desc: Per-biome multiplier on loot stage so tougher biomes yield extra-good loot. Works alongside Biome Progression.

sandbox.name: POITierLSModifier
sandbox.label: POI Tier Loot Stage Multiplier
sandbox.desc: Adjusts loot stage based on a POI's difficulty tier, so harder dungeons reward better loot.

sandbox.name: GlobalTSModifier
sandbox.label: Global Trader Stage Multiplier
sandbox.desc: Global multiplier on trader stage, which sets the range and quality of trader stock. Higher = high-tier trader items unlock earlier.

sandbox.name: DayNightLength
sandbox.label: Day Length (24h Cycle)
sandbox.desc: Real-time minutes for one full in-game 24-hour cycle. Longer cycles stretch out both days and nights.

sandbox.name: DayLightLength
sandbox.label: Daylight Hours
sandbox.desc: How many of the 24 in-game hours are daylight. More daylight shortens the dangerous night accordingly.
sandbox.example: Viel Tageslicht lässt nur eine kurze Nacht uebrig

sandbox.name: AllowMap
sandbox.label: Allow Map
sandbox.desc: Allows or disables use of the in-game map. Off = you must navigate without a map overview, a hardcore navigation challenge.

sandbox.name: AllowCompass
sandbox.label: Allow Compass
sandbox.desc: Toggles the compass bar at the top of the screen. Without it, direction-finding and following quest markers gets noticeably harder.

sandbox.name: AllowScreenMarkers
sandbox.label: Allow Screen Markers
sandbox.desc: Controls whether quest, trader, and other objective markers show on screen and compass. Off makes navigating to objectives much more challenging.

sandbox.name: ShowLocationInfo
sandbox.label: Show Location Info
sandbox.desc: Shows or hides location and area-name popups when you enter a new region. Display-only with no gameplay effect.

sandbox.name: ShowDayTime
sandbox.label: Show Day & Time
sandbox.desc: Toggles the current day and clock display in the HUD. Off means judging the time of day by the sky instead.

sandbox.name: WorkstationsInTheWild
sandbox.label: Workstations In The Wild
sandbox.desc: Sets whether pre-placed workstations in POIs and the world (forge, workbench, chem station, etc.) can be used. Off forces you to build every station yourself.

sandbox.name: MaxTechType
sandbox.label: Max Tech Tier
sandbox.desc: Caps the highest technology tier of items and recipes available in the world. Lower values force a more primitive playstyle.

sandbox.name: LootRespawnDays
sandbox.label: Loot Respawn Days
sandbox.desc: How many days before already-searched loot containers refill with new loot. Higher = slower restocking, so you must keep exploring fresh areas.

sandbox.name: LootTimer
sandbox.label: Loot Search Timer
sandbox.desc: Adds a delay when opening loot containers so searching takes a moment. On makes frantic looting under attack riskier.

sandbox.name: LootMaxTier
sandbox.label: Max Loot Tier
sandbox.desc: Caps the highest quality tier of items that can drop from loot. Low values prevent top-tier finds entirely, pushing you toward crafting or the trader.

sandbox.name: GlobalLootCount
sandbox.label: Global Loot Amount
sandbox.desc: Global multiplier for how much loot containers hold on average. Higher = fuller containers and faster resource gathering.

sandbox.name: FoodLootCount
sandbox.label: Food Loot Amount
sandbox.desc: Multiplier specifically for food found in containers. Lower values intensify the hunger survival pressure.

sandbox.name: DrinkLootCount
sandbox.label: Drink Loot Amount
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for drinks found in loot. Lower values make staying hydrated tougher.

sandbox.name: MedicalLootCount
sandbox.label: Medical Loot Amount
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for medical items like bandages and first aid kits in loot. Lower values make healing items scarce and precious.

sandbox.name: AmmoLootCount
sandbox.label: Ammo Loot Amount
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for ammunition found in loot. Lower values force you to conserve ammo or craft your own.

sandbox.name: ResourceLootCount
sandbox.label: Resource Loot Count
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for the amount of raw resources and building materials found in loot containers. Higher = faster supply stockpiles from looting and less time spent mining.

sandbox.name: ArmorLootCount
sandbox.label: Armor Loot Count
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how much armor you find in loot. Lower = armor is scarce and crafting your own matters more.

sandbox.name: MeleeLootCount
sandbox.label: Melee Weapon Loot Count
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how many melee weapons drop as loot. Affects how easily you find clubs, blades and sledges instead of crafting them.

sandbox.name: RangedLootCount
sandbox.label: Ranged Weapon Loot Count
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how many guns and bows appear in loot. Lower = firearms are rare and ammo discipline matters more.

sandbox.name: DukesLootCount
sandbox.label: Dukes Loot Count
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how many Dukes (the in-game currency) you find in containers. Higher = you can shop at traders sooner.

sandbox.name: CraftingMagazinesLootCount
sandbox.label: Crafting Magazine Loot Count
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how many crafting and skill magazines drop. This directly sets the pace of magazine-based crafting progression.
sandbox.example: Niedrige Werte verlangsamen den Rezept-Fortschritt drastisch

sandbox.name: TreasureMapChance
sandbox.label: Treasure Map Chance
sandbox.desc: Chance to find a buried-supply treasure map while looting. Higher = more dig-up treasure hunts.

sandbox.name: LootBagChance
sandbox.label: Loot Bag Drop Chance
sandbox.desc: Chance that a killed zombie drops a loot bag. Higher = fighting is rewarded with more drops.

sandbox.name: CropOutput
sandbox.label: Crop Harvest Yield
sandbox.desc: How many crops a fully grown plant gives when harvested. Higher = farming feeds you with fewer plants.

sandbox.name: SeedDropOutput
sandbox.label: Seed Return Yield
sandbox.desc: How many seeds you get back when harvesting, letting you replant without crafting. Higher = self-sustaining farms.

sandbox.name: CropGrowthSpeed
sandbox.label: Crop Growth Speed
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how fast planted crops grow to harvest. Higher = shorter wait before you can harvest food.

sandbox.name: BackpackCrafting
sandbox.label: Backpack Crafting
sandbox.desc: Controls what you can craft directly from your inventory without a workstation. Restricting it forces you to rely on workbenches, forges and other stations.

sandbox.name: WorkstationCrafting
sandbox.label: Workstation Crafting
sandbox.desc: Controls which recipes require a dedicated workstation (forge, workbench, chem station). Sets how much crafting depends on building and powering stations.

sandbox.name: CraftingProgression
sandbox.label: Crafting Progression
sandbox.desc: Turns the magazine-based crafting unlock system on or off. Off enables a classic no-magazine playstyle where recipes unlock differently.
sandbox.example: Aenderung kippt das gesamte Fortschrittssystem der Welt

sandbox.name: CraftingTime
sandbox.label: Crafting Time
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how long crafting takes. Higher = slower production; lower = faster.

sandbox.name: CraftingInput
sandbox.label: Crafting Cost
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how many ingredients a recipe consumes. Lower = cheaper crafting; higher = more material-hungry.

sandbox.name: CraftingOutput
sandbox.label: Crafting Output
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how many items come out per craft. Higher = more items per crafting action.

sandbox.name: CraftingMaxTier
sandbox.label: Max Craftable Tier
sandbox.desc: Caps the highest quality tier you can craft yourself. Anything above must be looted or bought from a trader.
sandbox.example: Niedrig gesetzt zwingt zum Looten statt Selbstbau.

sandbox.name: MiningOutput
sandbox.label: Mining Yield
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for resources gained from mining blocks (stone, ore, wood). Higher = more output per hit.

sandbox.name: HarvestingOutput
sandbox.label: Harvest Yield
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for what you gather from sources like plants, animal carcasses and salvage. Higher = more material per harvest.

sandbox.name: ScrappingOutput
sandbox.label: Scrapping Yield
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for materials returned when scrapping items. Higher = more resources recovered from junk.

sandbox.name: SmeltingType
sandbox.label: Smelting Mode
sandbox.desc: Sets how the forge smelts materials into usable resource, controlling the smelting rules and behavior.

sandbox.name: DewCollectorTime
sandbox.label: Dew Collector Time
sandbox.desc: How long a Dew Collector takes to produce one unit of water. Lower = faster water supply.

sandbox.name: DewCollectorOutput
sandbox.label: Dew Collector Output
sandbox.desc: How much a Dew Collector yields per cycle. Higher = more water per run.

sandbox.name: DewCollectorInput
sandbox.label: Dew Collector Input
sandbox.desc: How much input material a Dew Collector consumes per cycle. Higher = more expensive to run.

sandbox.name: ApiaryTime
sandbox.label: Apiary Time
sandbox.desc: How long a beehive (Apiary) takes per production cycle. Lower = faster honey supply.

sandbox.name: ApiaryOutput
sandbox.label: Apiary Output
sandbox.desc: How much a beehive yields per cycle. Higher = more honey per run.

sandbox.name: ApiaryInput
sandbox.label: Apiary Input
sandbox.desc: How much input a beehive consumes per cycle. Higher = more expensive to run.

sandbox.name: RepairTypes
sandbox.label: Repair Methods
sandbox.desc: Sets which repair methods are allowed and how repairing items works overall.

sandbox.name: MaxDegradationAmount
sandbox.label: Repair Wear
sandbox.desc: How much an item's maximum durability permanently drops with each repair. Higher = gear wears out for good over time.
sandbox.example: Hoch + Drop-On-Death=Alles macht Ausruestung sehr verganglich.

sandbox.name: PointsPerMagazine
sandbox.label: Points Per Magazine
sandbox.desc: How many crafting-progress points each crafting magazine you read grants. Higher = unlock recipes and quality tiers with fewer magazines.
sandbox.example: Achtung: veraendert die Zuordnung Magazinanzahl zu Crafting-Level.

sandbox.name: SkillGainRate
sandbox.label: Skill Gain Rate
sandbox.desc: Global multiplier for how fast skills and crafting progress rise. Higher = faster progression.

sandbox.name: SkillPointsPerLevel
sandbox.label: Skill Points Per Level
sandbox.desc: How many skill points you get per character level-up. Higher = unlock perks and attributes faster.
sandbox.example: Hoch macht das Spiel deutlich leichter (mehr Perks frueher).

sandbox.name: QuestsEnabled
sandbox.label: Quests Enabled
sandbox.desc: Master switch for the whole quest system. Off = no trader or POI quests at all.

sandbox.name: IntroQuestEnabled
sandbox.label: Intro Quest
sandbox.desc: Whether the starter/tutorial quest line that leads you to the first trader is active.

sandbox.name: TraderToTraderQuestsEnabled
sandbox.label: Trader-To-Trader Quests
sandbox.desc: Whether traders can hand out quests whose objective is another trader (trade-route jobs).

sandbox.name: StarterSkillPoints
sandbox.label: Starting Skill Points
sandbox.desc: How many skill points a new character begins with. Higher = a stronger start.

sandbox.name: QuestsPerTier
sandbox.label: Quests Per Tier
sandbox.desc: How many quests of one tier you must finish before the next trader quest tier unlocks. Higher = slower quest-rank climb.

sandbox.name: QuestProgressionDailyLimit
sandbox.label: Daily Quest Limit
sandbox.desc: Caps how many quests per day count toward tier progress. Prevents rushing the quest rank in a single day.

sandbox.name: BuriedQuestsEnabled
sandbox.label: Buried Supply Quests
sandbox.desc: Turns the buried-supply (dig-up treasure) quests on or off.

sandbox.name: POIQuestsEnabled
sandbox.label: POI Quests
sandbox.desc: Whether POI clear and fetch quests inside buildings are available.

sandbox.name: TraderDialog
sandbox.label: Trader Dialog
sandbox.desc: Whether and how much trader dialog text is shown when you talk to a trader.

sandbox.name: TraderHours
sandbox.label: Trader Hours
sandbox.desc: The daily time window when traders are open and enterable. Outside those hours the trader stays closed.

sandbox.name: TradersEnabled
sandbox.label: Traders Enabled
sandbox.desc: Master switch for all trader NPCs and their trading posts.
sandbox.example: Aus entfernt eine der wichtigsten Versorgungs- und Geldquellen.

sandbox.name: VendingEnabled
sandbox.label: Vending Machines Enabled
sandbox.desc: Turns world vending machines on or off, where you can buy independently of a trader.

sandbox.name: TraderSellPrices
sandbox.label: Trader Sell Prices
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for the prices traders charge you for goods. Higher = more expensive to buy.

sandbox.name: TraderBuyPrices
sandbox.label: Trader Buy Prices
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for the prices traders pay you for goods. Higher = more Dukes when selling.

sandbox.name: TraderProtection
sandbox.label: Trader Protection Zone
sandbox.desc: The safe zone around a trading post where enemies don't spawn and you can't be attacked. Also affects PvP safety near the trader.
sandbox.example: Wichtig auf PvP-Servern: regelt, ob der Haendler Safezone ist.

sandbox.name: TraderResetInterval
sandbox.label: Trader Restock Interval
sandbox.desc: How many days until a trader regenerates its stock. Lower = fresher offers more often.

sandbox.name: TraderItemAbundance
sandbox.label: Trader Stock Abundance
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for the amount and variety a trader keeps in stock. Higher = a fuller shop.

sandbox.name: TraderBuyLimit
sandbox.label: Trader Buy Limit
sandbox.desc: Caps how much you can buy from a trader per reset period. Stops you from clearing the whole stock at once.

sandbox.name: TraderMaxTier
sandbox.label: Trader Max Tier
sandbox.desc: The highest item/quality tier a trader is allowed to offer. Lower = only weaker gear is buyable.

sandbox.name: VendingResetInterval
sandbox.label: Vending Restock Interval
sandbox.desc: How many days until vending machines refill their stock. Lower = more frequent restocks.

sandbox.name: VendingItemAbundance
sandbox.label: Vending Stock Abundance
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how much vending machines carry. Higher = more buyable items per machine.

sandbox.name: ChallengesEnabled
sandbox.label: Challenges Enabled
sandbox.desc: Turns the Challenges system on or off, which grants rewards and XP for completing tasks.

sandbox.name: IntroChallengesEnabled
sandbox.label: Intro Challenges
sandbox.desc: Whether the introductory tutorial challenges that teach core mechanics are active.

sandbox.name: VehicleFuelUsage
sandbox.label: Vehicle Fuel Usage
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for how much fuel vehicles burn. Higher = vehicles guzzle more gas per distance.

sandbox.name: VehicleEntityDamage
sandbox.label: Vehicle Damage To Enemies
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for the damage a vehicle deals when ramming enemies. Higher = run zombies over more easily.

sandbox.name: VehicleBlockDamage
sandbox.label: Vehicle Block Damage
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for the damage a vehicle does to blocks and structures on impact. Higher = ram through walls more easily.

sandbox.name: VehicleSelfDamage
sandbox.label: Vehicle Self Damage
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for the damage a vehicle takes from collisions. Lower = vehicles survive crashes better.

sandbox.name: ElectricalOutput
sandbox.label: Electrical Output
sandbox.desc: Multiplier for the power output of electrical sources like generators and solar banks. Higher = more devices powered per source.

sandbox.name: SillyCelebrate
sandbox.label: Kill Confetti
sandbox.desc: Joke mode where killed zombies burst into exaggerated celebration effects. Purely cosmetic, no gameplay impact.

sandbox.name: SillyBigHeads
sandbox.label: Big Heads
sandbox.desc: Joke mode that gives everyone (or the zombies) oversized heads. Purely visual, no balance impact.

sandbox.name: SillyTinyZombies
sandbox.label: Tiny Zombies
sandbox.desc: Joke mode that shrinks zombies to tiny size. Purely visual, though it can make headshots harder.

sandbox.name: SillySounds
sandbox.label: Silly Sounds
sandbox.desc: Joke mode that replaces game sound effects with goofy noises. Pure audio fun, no gameplay impact.

sandbox.name: SillyLowGravity
sandbox.label: Low Gravity
sandbox.desc: Joke mode that lowers gravity, so players, enemies and objects jump higher and fall more slowly. Changes the feel of all movement; purely for fun, no balance impact.

sandbox.name: SillyBlackandWhite
sandbox.label: Black & White
sandbox.desc: Joke mode that renders everything in black and white. A pure visual filter with no gameplay effect.

plain.name: GameDifficulty
plain.label: Game Difficulty
plain.desc: Global difficulty level. Higher = zombies have more health and you deal less / take more damage. Insane = you deal 50% damage while enemies deal 250%.

plain.name: PlayerKillingMode
plain.label: Player Killing (PvP) Mode
plain.desc: Controls whether and how players can damage each other. Options range from no PvP, to allies only, to strafe-only, to full free-for-all (Kill Everyone).

plain.name: ServerVisibility
plain.label: Server Visibility
plain.desc: Who can see your server in the browser list: Hidden (invite-only via IP), Friends Only, or Public.

plain.name: ServerMaxPlayerCount
plain.label: Max Players
plain.desc: Maximum number of players allowed on the server at the same time. Higher = more simultaneous players, but greater load on the host.

plain.name: LandClaimSize
plain.label: Land Claim Size
plain.desc: Edge length (in blocks) of the protected area around a Land Claim Block. Higher = a larger zone where your base blocks are reinforced and other players cannot build or grief.

plain.name: LandClaimDeadZone
plain.label: Land Claim Spacing
plain.desc: Minimum distance required between Land Claim Blocks owned by different players. Higher = players must space their claims farther apart.

plain.name: LandClaimExpiryTime
plain.label: Land Claim Expiry (Days)
plain.desc: Number of consecutive offline days before an owner's Land Claim expires and loses its protection. Higher = claims stay protected longer while you are away.

plain.name: LandClaimOnlineDurabilityModifier
plain.label: Claim Hardness (Owner Online)
plain.desc: Block hardness multiplier inside a claim while its owner is online. Higher = base blocks are much tougher to destroy, improving defense against raids.

plain.name: LandClaimOfflineDurabilityModifier
plain.label: Claim Hardness (Owner Offline)
plain.desc: Block hardness multiplier inside a claim while its owner is offline. Higher = your base resists damage better when you are not logged in; set low to allow offline raiding.

plain.name: BedrollDeadZoneSize
plain.label: Bedroll Safe Zone
plain.desc: Radius around your bedroll in which enemies will not spawn. Higher = a larger safe area around your spawn point and base.

plain.name: BedrollExpiryTime
plain.label: Bedroll Expiry (Days)
plain.desc: Number of days an unused bedroll stays active before it expires and stops working as a spawn point. Higher = your respawn point persists longer without visiting it.

plain.name: PartySharedKillRange
plain.label: Party Shared Kill Range (m)
plain.desc: Distance in meters within which party members share XP and quest credit for kills. Higher = teammates can spread out farther and still benefit from each other's kills.

plain.name: BuildCreate
plain.label: Creative Mode
plain.desc: Allows access to the creative menu and its free items (a cheat). On = players can spawn any block or item; leave off for a legitimate survival playthrough.

plain.name: PersistentPlayerProfiles
plain.label: Require Profile Login
plain.desc: When on, only players whose profiles have been pre-authorized (whitelisted) may join. Use to restrict the server to invited players only.

plain.name: ServerAllowCrossplay
plain.label: Crossplay
plain.desc: Allows players on different platforms (PC and consoles) to join the same server. Disable to restrict the game to a single platform.

perks.name: Dead Eye
perks.desc: Erhöht Schaden und Schwachpunkt-Treffer mit Gewehren und Scharfschützengewehren.

perks.name: Demolitions Expert
perks.desc: Erhöht Schaden und Wirkungsradius von Sprengstoffen und Granaten.

perks.name: Spear Master
perks.desc: Erhöht Schaden und Effizienz im Umgang mit Speeren.

perks.name: The Infiltrator
ranks: Fallen und Schlösser kaum noch ein Hindernis
perks.desc: Verbessert Schlösserknacken und reduziert das Auslösen von Fallen.

perks.name: Animal Tracker
perks.desc: Ortet Tiere in der Umgebung für leichtere Jagd.

perks.name: The Penetrator
perks.desc: Erhöht die Durchschlagskraft gegen Panzerung und Deckung.

perks.name: Treasure Hunter
perks.desc: Erleichtert das Auffinden vergrabener Schätze.

perks.name: Salvage Operations

perks.name: Perception Mastery
perks.desc: Verstärkt alle Perception-Fähigkeiten global.

perks.name: Boomstick
perks.desc: Erhöht Schaden und Effizienz von Schrotflinten.

perks.name: Pummel Pete
perks.desc: Erhöht Schaden und Effizienz von Keulen.

perks.name: Skull Crusher

perks.name: Grand Slam
ranks: Gegner fliegen am zuverlässigsten zu Boden

perks.name: Big and Fast

perks.name: Pack Mule
perks.desc: Erhöht das Inventar-Trageinventar und die Tragkapazität.

perks.name: Strength Mastery
perks.desc: Verstärkt alle Strength-Fähigkeiten global.

perks.name: Miner 69'er
perks.desc: Erhöht Schaden und Geschwindigkeit von Abbauwerkzeugen.

perks.name: Mother Lode
perks.desc: Erhöht die Rohstoffausbeute beim Abbauen.

perks.name: Junk Miner
perks.desc: Erhöht die Ausbeute von Junk-Rohstoffen aus zerstörbaren Objekten.

perks.name: The Brawler
perks.desc: Erhöht Schaden und Effizienz im waffenlosen Faustkampf.

perks.name: Machine Gunner
perks.desc: Erhöht Schaden und Kontrolle von Maschinengewehren.

perks.name: Lightning Hands
perks.desc: Erhöht die Angriffsgeschwindigkeit von Faustwaffen.

perks.name: The Huntsman
ranks: Mehr Fleisch und Leder beim Ausweiden
perks.desc: Erhöht die Ausbeute von Fleisch und Leder bei der Jagd.

perks.name: Pain Tolerance
perks.desc: Reduziert die Wirkung von Betäubung und Niederschlag durch Gegner.

perks.name: Fortitude Mastery
perks.desc: Verstärkt alle Fortitude-Fähigkeiten global.

perks.name: Siphoning Strikes

perks.name: Healing Factor
perks.desc: Beschleunigt die natürliche Regeneration der Lebenspunkte.

perks.name: Iron Gut
perks.desc: Reduziert den Verbrauch von Nahrung und Wasser.

perks.name: Rule 1: Cardio
perks.desc: Erhöht maximale Stamina und deren Regeneration.

perks.name: Archery
perks.desc: Erhöht Schaden und Effizienz von Bögen und Armbrüsten.

perks.name: Gunslinger
perks.desc: Erhöht Schaden und Handling von Pistolen und Maschinenpistolen.

perks.name: Deep Cuts
perks.desc: Erhöht Klingenschaden und verstärkt Blutungseffekte.

perks.name: Whirlwind

perks.name: Run and Gun

perks.name: Hard Target

perks.name: Parkour

perks.name: Agility Mastery

perks.name: Physician

perks.name: Intellect Mastery

perks.name: Heavy Armor

perks.name: Master Chef

perks.name: Lucky Looter

categories.name: Waffen
categories.desc: Schuss-, Nah- und Wurfwaffen aller Qualitätsstufen (Tier 0-6). Hochwertige Stücke stammen aus militärischen und waffenspezifischen Containern.
categories.examples: Pistole, Magnum .44, AK-47, Tactical AR, M60, Jagdgewehr, Pump-Shotgun, Bogen/Armbrust, Knochen-/Stahlmesser, Sledgehammer, Machete

categories.name: Munition
categories.desc: Patronen, Pfeile, Bolzen und Sprengmunition. Gdebündelt in Muni-Kisten, Polizeiwagen und im Munitions-Container; Sprengstoff separat.
categories.examples: 9mm, 7.62mm, .44, Schrot, Pfeile/Bolzen, Rohrbomben, Dynamit, Molotow, Raketen

categories.name: Werkzeuge
categories.desc: Sammel-, Bau- und Reparatur-Werkzeuge sowie Werkzeug-Mods. Hauptsächlich in Werkzeugkisten, Garagen und 'Working Stiffs'-POIs.
categories.examples: Steinaxt, Auger, Kettensäge, Nagelpistole, Schaufel, Spitzhacke, Wrench, Reparaturkit, Werkzeug-Mods

categories.name: Medizin
categories.desc: Heil- und Buff-Items gegen Verletzungen, Infektion und Statuswerte. Aus Apotheken-POIs (Pop-N-Pills), Krankenhaus-Containern und Erste-Hilfe-Behältern.
categories.examples: Verband, Erste-Hilfe-Verband/-Kit, Schmerzmittel, Antibiotika, Honig, Vitamine, Blutbeutel, Medikamenten-Stapel

categories.name: Nahrung
categories.desc: Essen, Getränke und Zutaten gegen Hunger/Durst. Aus Kühlschränken, Öfen, Mikrowellen, Vorratskisten und Lebensmittel-POIs (Shamway).
categories.examples: Konservendosen, abgepacktes Essen, Getränke (Cola/Bier), Kaffee, Wasser, MegaCrush, rohe Zutaten, Mais/Super-Corn

categories.name: Ressourcen
categories.desc: Crafting-Rohstoffe und Bauteile. Aus Müll/Junk-Containern, Schränken, kollabierten Workstations und Bau-POIs.
categories.examples: Schrott-Eisen/-Polymer, Mechanische/Elektrische Teile, Federn, Klebstoff, Säure, Chemikalien (Pottasche/Nitrat/Kohle), Münzen/Casino-Coins

categories.name: Rüstung
categories.desc: Rüstungssets und Kleidung nach Outfit-Klassen (Tier 0-2+ in Loot). Aus Schränken, Kleiderständern, Rüstungs-Racks und klassenspezifischen Twitch-Boxen.
categories.examples: Lumberjack, Nomad, Enforcer, Commando, Miner, Ranger, Nerd, Raider, Scavenger, Biker, Athletic; Schuhe, Kleidung

categories.name: Magazine
categories.desc: Skill-/Crafting-Magazine — der Haupt-Progressionspfad nach 1.0. Lesen schaltet Rezepte und Perk-Stufen frei. Aus Bücherregalen, Buchstapeln, Briefkästen und Quest-Belohnungen.
categories.examples: Crafting-Magazin-Serien (z.B. Forge Ahead, Needle & Thread, Ranged Weapons), Einzelbücher, Schemata; questRewardSkillMagazines

categories.name: Schätze
categories.desc: Hochwertige/seltene Beute aus versteckten oder vergrabenen Quellen sowie Endgame-Behältern. Oft an Quests, Schatzkarten oder Safe-Knacken gekoppelt.
categories.examples: Vergrabener Schatz, Hidden Stash, Tresor-Inhalt (Gun Safe/Shamway Safe), Dukaten, ATM-Loot, Air-Drop, gehärtete Kisten T4/T5

categories.name: Fahrzeugteile
categories.desc: Bauteile zum Crafting von Fahrzeugen plus Treibstoff. Aus Auto-Wracks, Armee-/Farm-Trucks, Garagen und Tankstellen (Pass-N-Gas).
categories.examples: Fahrradteile, Minibike-/Motorrad-/4x4-/Gyrocopter-Teile, Motor, Akku, Reifen/Räder, Benzin, Öl

lootstageTable.note: Spielanfang: nur graue/braune Qualität, niedriger Quality-Wert. Mods sehr selten. Kuratiert: exakte Schwellen sind in loot.xml über probability-templates gestaffelt.
Frühe Spielphase. Erste Tier-1/2-Waffen und einfache Werkzeuge. Magazine sind hier der Haupt-Fortschritt, nicht das Looten von High-Tier-Gear.
Mittlere Phase. Tier-3-Gear und mehr Mods erscheinen. Kuratiert: Übergangsband, kein harter Cut.
Tier-4-Items (blau/lila) werden realistisch. Gehärtete/militärische Container lohnen sich stärker.
Spätphase. Tier-5-Drops häufiger; Endgame-Container (Gun Safe, Air-Drop, gehärtete T4/T5) liefern Spitzen-Gear.
Höchste Loot-Stage. Tier-6 ist die Spitze; Legendary-Teile bzw. Top-Quality nur bei sehr hoher Loot-Stage + Lucky-Looter-Bonus. Kuratiert: kein garantierter Drop.

whereFound.what: Waffen & Munition (gehobene Qualität): munitions, militaryFootlocker, militaryShelves, militaryGoods, gunSafe, gunRack, ammoDisplay, weaponsBagSmall/Large, weaponTestLoot
whereFound.where: Militär-POIs (Army Camp, Bunker), Waffenläden (Shotgun Messiah), Polizeistationen; Polizeiwagen (policeCars/policeCarsBonus) für Muni

whereFound.what: Werkzeuge & Bauteile: toolBox, rollingToolBox, garage, workingStiffsCrate/Shelves/Truck, constructionCrate, savageCountryCrate
Medizin: medicalPillCase, medicalPileSmall/Medium/Large, medicineCabinet, coolerMedical, popnpillsCrate/Shelves, zPackNurse/Lab
Nahrung & Getränke: refrigerator(+retro/steel), oven, microwave, foodPileSmall/Med/Large, shamwayProduce/Freezer/Drinks, beerCooler, sodaFountain, coffeeMaker, charcoalGrill
whereFound.where: Küchen in Häusern, Shamway-Supermärkte, Restaurants/Fast-Food, Diner, Bars

whereFound.what: Ressourcen & Chemie: junk, garbage, dumpster, cupboard, closet, chemPileSmall/Med/Large, collapsedForge/Workbench/ChemistryStation/CementMixer
whereFound.where: Müllbehälter überall, Schränke in Häusern, kollabierte Workstations in zerstörten/Werkstatt-POIs

whereFound.what: Magazine & Bücher: bookcase, bookShelves, bookPile, singleBooks, crackabookCrate/Shelves, mailbox, PObox, nightstand
Rüstung & Kleidung: armorRack, clothesRacks, clothesShelves, closet, shoes, laundryBasket
Schätze & Tresore: buriedTreasure, buriedSuppliesT1/T2, hiddenStash, smallSafes, gunSafe, shamwaySafe, atmLoot, airDrop, hardenedChestT4/T5, reinforcedChestT1-T3
whereFound.where: Vergrabene Schätze (Schatzkarten/Quests), versteckte Stashes, Safes in POIs, Geldautomaten, Air-Drops, gehärtete Endgame-Kisten in High-Tier-POIs

whereFound.what: Fahrzeugteile & Treibstoff: vehicleBicycle/Minibike/Motorcycle/4x4Truck/Gyrocopter, armyTruck, farmTruck, cars, gasPump, gasBarrel(+Large), passngasCrate

whereFound.where: Infested-Quest-POIs (höhere Loot-Stage/Bonus-Tier als normale Container), Fetch-Quest-Satchel, Bandit-Drops

tips: Lucky Looter (Perception) erhöht Loot-Stage und Drop-Qualität — jede Perk-Stufe pusht spürbar bessere Quality-Tiers; das Outfit-Set 'Scavenger/Rogue' und der Aloe-/Glasses-Mod stapeln zusätzlich.
Loot-Stage ist NICHT Gamestage: Sie skaliert mit Spielerlevel, Lucky Looter, Tageszeit, getragener Ausrüstung und dem Biome-Multiplikator. Höhere Loot-Stage = höhere Tiers, nicht nur 'mehr Zeug'.
Biome × Loot: Härtere Biome (Wasteland > Burnt Forest/Snow > Desert > Pine Forest) geben einen Loot-Stage-Bonus. Im Wasteland looten lohnt sich für besseres Gear deutlich.
Nach 1.0/V3.0 kommt Crafting-Fortschritt fast nur über Skill-/Crafting-Magazine — Crack-a-Book-POIs, Bücherregale und Briefkästen gezielt clearen, nicht nur Waffenkisten.
Tresore (gunSafe, smallSafes, shamwaySafe) und gehärtete Kisten T4/T5 sind die zuverlässigsten High-Tier-Quellen am POI-Ende ('Loot-Room'); volles POI-Clearen für die End-Belohnung lohnt fast immer.
Infested-Quests geben gegenüber normalen Behältern einen Loot-Tier-Bonus und sind die effizienteste Quelle für Top-Gear bei hoher Loot-Stage — kuratiert: genaue Boni variieren je Version/Difficulty.
